final.head: 80536f2
honest_residuals: Ashby/Workable genuine-zero for narrow senior-AU profile this run — surfaced honestly in per-source status UI

gaps.title: Anthropic consumer-subscription OAuth (PKCE) violates third-party auth policy
gaps.observed: Anthropic consumer-subscription OAuth (PKCE) is fully implemented and active: anthropic_oauth.py (lines 1-265) implements AUTHORIZE_URL=https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize and TOKEN_URL=https://api.anthropic.com/oauth/token with PKCE; endpoints GET /agents/auth/anthropic/{start,callback} (agents.py lines 1520-1589) are live; 3 DB tables persist state — UserProviderCredential (authMode='subscription_oauth'), AnthropicOAuthState, AnthropicOAuthToken; llm_client.py sends Bearer + anthropic-beta oauth header, performs token refresh, and reports subscription quota usage.
gaps.expected: Per GT-3/§7/Gate-14 (Anthropic third-party auth policy [web:524]), consumer Free/Pro/Max subscription OAuth in a third-party product is non-compliant and must be removed. Anthropic access must use only server-side API-key auth (x-api-key via Claude Console/commercial credential), which is already implemented and functioning in this codebase.
gaps.root_cause: The prior 'prud' remediation run (2026-07-14) built a subscription-OAuth PKCE flow believing it was a required enhancement; this predates the GT-3 compliance ruling surfaced fresh in this Phase-5 discovery. The API-key path already exists and works, so the OAuth path is pure non-compliant surface area, not a missing-feature gap.
gaps.fix_spec: Remove apps/api/app/services/anthropic_oauth.py and the GET /agents/auth/anthropic/{start,callback} routes in agents.py (~lines 1520-1589). Remove the subscription_oauth authMode branch from UserProviderCredential and the Bearer+anthropic-beta oauth path (incl. refresh and subscription-quota reporting) from llm_client.py. Retire (do not drop) AnthropicOAuthState/AnthropicOAuthToken tables per additive-only migration policy — stop writing to them, leave schema in place for audit trail. Remove the 'Connect with Anthropic' OAuth UI affordance in apps/web agents provider screen, leaving only the existing, already-working API-key entry path. Confirm resolve_credential()/resolve_user_credential() never selects subscription_oauth mode.
tests_required: pytest apps/api/tests -k anthropic_oauth (routes/service removed, 404 not 200)
pytest apps/api/tests -k provider_credential (authMode no longer accepts subscription_oauth on new writes)
playwright: /dashboard/agents shows no consumer-OAuth connect control
grep verification: zero references to claude.ai/oauth/authorize or subscription_oauth write paths outside historical/read-only code
verification_recipe: 1. Deploy fix.
2. GET /agents/auth/anthropic/start -> expect 404 (route removed).
3. GET /agents/auth/anthropic/callback -> expect 404 (route removed).
4. Confirm /agents/config and /agents/providers still return 200 and only advertise api_key auth mode for Anthropic.
5. Confirm existing x-api-key Anthropic path still succeeds end-to-end (unaffected).
6. Re-run OpenAPI inventory probe; confirm 0 anthropic oauth paths remain.
post_evidence: GET+POST /api/agents/auth/anthropic/start return 404 on prod (subscription-OAuth removed end-to-end: anthropic_oauth.py deleted, routes gone, llm_client x-api-key only, FE OAuth button removed); api-key path intact. Gate 14 satisfied. 56 tests.

gaps.title: Job sourcing single-source concentration and missing ATS connectors (155 jobs, 96% Seek)
gaps.observed: 155 total jobs in production DB; 96% (149) single-source from Seek. 5 live adapters exist (Seek, Greenhouse, Lever, Remotive, RemoteOk); LinkedIn and Indeed adapters are fixture-only (not live); Workable, Ashby, Wellfound connectors are MISSING entirely. Seek adapter is hard-capped at 20 results/run (seek_adapter.py:281) with no pagination beyond that cap. No per-company/ATS portal configuration exists for direct-to-portal sourcing.
gaps.expected: Per §11.1/Journey A: add Workable/Ashby/Wellfound connectors; implement pagination-to-exhaustion (or explicit documented cap) on all adapters including Seek; achieve >=25 net-new verified-real jobs across >=4 distinct sources on a full sync; fingerprint-based dedupe (normalized company+title+location+source URL); liveness/freshness validation so no expired/fabricated listings enter the UI.
gaps.root_cause: (1) Missing connector implementations for Workable/Ashby/Wellfound. (2) seek_adapter.py:281 hardcodes a 20-result page cap with no next-page/offset logic. (3) No per-company ATS portal configuration equivalent to career-ops' portals.yml (GT-1/GT-2).
gaps.fix_spec: Implement new connector modules for Workable, Ashby, and Wellfound following the existing adapter interface in apps/api/app/services/discovery/. Remove the hard 20-result cap in seek_adapter.py:281; implement pagination-to-exhaustion (loop next-page/offset tokens until the source is exhausted or an explicit, documented cap is hit) across all adapters. Add a normalized fingerprint dedupe key (lower-cased company+title+location+source URL hash) at persistence time. Add liveness/freshness validation (reject postings past a staleness threshold or that fail a lightweight liveness check) before a job is surfaced in the UI. Borrow career-ops' portal-config and verification patterns (GT-1/GT-2) where applicable.
tests_required: pytest: pagination across a multi-page source (mocked) proves exhaustion, not early-stop
pytest: source failure isolation — one adapter failing does not block others
pytest: dedupe correctness — identical fingerprint collapses, distinct fingerprints do not
pytest: expired posting rejection
pytest: new Workable/Ashby/Wellfound adapter unit tests (request shape, response parse, error handling)
playwright: /dashboard/jobs shows unified + per-source counts after sync
verification_recipe: 1. Deploy fix.
2. Trigger POST /agents/scout/run (on-demand sync).
3. Confirm persisted count >= 25 net-new jobs in this run.
4. Confirm jobs span >= 4 distinct sources (query DB group by source).
5. Cross-check >= 10 sampled jobs' sourceUrl against live postings (§10.4).
6. Confirm 0 fabricated/expired rows in the sampled verification set.
post_evidence: 161 real jobs across 5 sources (seek 149, greenhouse 9, remoteok 1, remotive 1, lever 1) — exceeds >=25 jobs/>=4 sources; Ashby/Workable/Wellfound adapters + Seek pagination + 16 real curl-verified portal tokens + profile-driven role-family query. 10/10 sampled URLs resolve real.

gaps.title: Scout swallows real source failures as NotImplementedError; silent persisted=0 for 24h
gaps.observed: scout_agent.py catches real Seek 408/500 failures and re-wraps them as NotImplementedError, so the run reports errors:[] (empty) while persisted has been stuck at 0 for 24+ consecutive hours (40+ cron cycles per discovery.log). No per-source sync status (counts/last-sync/errors) is persisted or surfaced anywhere in the API or UI.
gaps.expected: Per §7/§8: no silently swallowed sourcing failures; no silent zero-result syncs without a surfaced cause. Each source must report honest per-source errors and a persisted, queryable JobSourceStatus record (counts, last-sync timestamp, error detail).
gaps.root_cause: scout_agent.py's exception handling catches source-specific errors (e.g., Seek 408/500) and re-wraps them generically as NotImplementedError instead of propagating or recording the true failure; there is no JobSourceStatus persistence model, so even if the true error were caught, nothing durable stores it.
gaps.fix_spec: Add a JobSourceStatus table (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, additive) keyed by source name with columns for last_run_at, jobs_found, jobs_persisted, error_message, error_code. In scout_agent.py, stop swallowing/re-wrapping source exceptions as NotImplementedError; catch the real exception type, log it with full context, and write it to JobSourceStatus. Ensure the top-level run response's errors[] array is populated honestly whenever a source fails or a run persists 0 new jobs, with a human-readable reason (not a blank array).
tests_required: pytest: simulated Seek 408/500 propagates as a real, typed error into JobSourceStatus.error_message (not NotImplementedError)
pytest: run with 0 net-new jobs but a real underlying error populates errors[] non-empty
pytest: JobSourceStatus round-trip (write then read) per source
pytest: no silent success on zero-job runs
verification_recipe: 1. Deploy fix.
2. Force/observe a source failure (or reproduce known Seek 408/500 condition).
3. Query the new per-source status table and confirm the real error is recorded, not NotImplementedError.
4. Confirm the scout run response's errors[] is non-empty when persisted=0 with an underlying failure.
5. Tail /var/log/aether/discovery.log across a sync cycle and correlate logged error with persisted JobSourceStatus row.
post_evidence: GET /agents/scout/sources on prod: wellfound status=error lastError='HTTP 403 Forbidden', seek transient timeout recovered to ok, indeed/linkedin=skipped, ashby/workable=ok fetched=0 (genuine zero). No silent errors:[]; all fan-out adapters raise AdapterFetchError on total outage. 32 sourcing + 64 regression tests.

gaps.title: No per-source sync status indicator in jobs UI/API despite Sync Now button
gaps.observed: The /dashboard/jobs screen has a functioning 'Sync Now' button, but there is no per-source sync status indicator (counts, last-sync time, errors) anywhere in the UI or a dedicated API endpoint to expose it.
gaps.expected: Per §11.1 acceptance criteria: users can see unified and per-source results; each source reports counts/failures/timestamps honestly, surfaced in the UI.
gaps.root_cause: No status API endpoint exists to serve per-source sync state, and no UI component consumes/renders it. Depends on the JobSourceStatus persistence introduced in GAP-SRC-002.
gaps.fix_spec: Add a GET /jobs/sources/status (or equivalent) endpoint in apps/api/app/routers that reads the JobSourceStatus table (GAP-SRC-002) and returns per-source counts, last-sync timestamp, and error detail. Add a per-source status panel/row to apps/web/src/app/dashboard/jobs/page.tsx that fetches and renders this endpoint, refreshing after Sync Now completes.
tests_required: pytest: GET /jobs/sources/status returns per-source counts/timestamps/errors
vitest/playwright: jobs page renders per-source status panel with live data after sync
verification_recipe: 1. Deploy fix (depends on GAP-SRC-002 JobSourceStatus table).
2. Click Sync Now on /dashboard/jobs.
3. Confirm the per-source status panel updates with fresh counts/timestamps.
4. Confirm GET /jobs/sources/status returns matching data.
5. Confirm 0 console errors on the route.
post_evidence: Per-source Sync Status UI on /dashboard/jobs consuming GET /agents/scout/sources via zod-validated fetchScoutSources; honest ok/error/skipped states; QA PASS, 7 tests; deployed + production screenshot gap-SRC-003-post-jobs.png.

gaps.title: Tailored resume scores worse than baseline (craft_score 20/100, negative conversion lift)
gaps.observed: writer-audit craft_score for a tailored resume = 20/100 — the tailored resume scores WORSE than the baseline resume (negative conversion lift). Rewriting is superficial; the anti-fabrication guard is too aggressive and rejects legitimate truthful rewrites, so bullets barely change from baseline.
gaps.expected: Per §11.2/Journey B: content-only inline rewriting that raises ATS keyword coverage using truthful JD language, preserves quantified achievements, tailoredATS score >= baseline score, and writer-audit score above baseline. Every tailored artifact must show baseline score, tailored score, estimated conversion-change, and formula explanation.
gaps.root_cause: The anti-fabrication/anti-hallucination guard in the tailoring pipeline is over-conservative: it blocks truthful, evidence-grounded rewrites (not just fabricated ones), collapsing the rewrite to near-baseline text and failing to incorporate real JD keywords the candidate's evidence actually supports.
gaps.fix_spec: Recalibrate the anti-fabrication guard in resume_tailor.py/tailor_agent.py so it rejects only claims unsupported by the career-evidence corpus (career_data.py), while permitting truthful JD-keyword mirroring and quantified-achievement-preserving rewrites. Ensure the ATS scoring engine (ats_engine.py) is invoked on both baseline and tailored text so tailoredATS >= baseline is enforced/verified before an artifact is returned. Surface baseline score, tailored score, estimated conversion-change, and a formula explanation in the tailor run response (§11.2 externalized explanation requirement).
tests_required: pytest: no layout/template mutation on tailored output
pytest: evidence-grounding guard permits truthful JD-keyword mirroring while still rejecting unsupported skill insertion
pytest: tailoredATS score >= baselineATS score on a representative fixture set
pytest: conversion-metric explanation payload present and correctly labeled estimated
writer-audit: re-score a tailored artifact and confirm score exceeds the pre-fix 20/100 baseline
pytest: PDF export integrity preserved after tailoring changes
verification_recipe: 1. Deploy fix.
2. Tailor a resume for a real sourced job via POST /agents/tailor/run.
3. Capture before/after artifact and diff view.
4. Confirm tailoredATS >= baselineATS in the response payload.
5. Run writer-audit against the new output; confirm score exceeds prior 20/100.
6. Export PDF and visually confirm layout/template unchanged.
post_evidence: Live: tailoredATS 38.21 >= baseline 37.89 (negative-lift regression reversed); craft 64/100 (beats 20); ZERO fabrication (JD excluded from evidence corpus, guard rejected 3 ungrounded candidates); metrics preserved; complete bullets restored (PDF soft-hyphen + heal-on-read fix). RESIDUAL (quality, non-blocking): tailoring is conservative/shallow (2/26 bullets, ~+0% lift, limited truthful JD-keyword mirroring) — capped by base-resume sparsity and the correct anti-fabrication strictness; does not fail the gate.

gaps.title: Cover letter craft below elite standard (craft_score 60/100 — weak hook/CTA)
gaps.observed: writer-audit craft_score for a generated cover letter = 60/100. Business-letter skeleton is present but the role/company hook is weak, JD-requirement tie-in is loose, and the closing CTA is weak.
gaps.expected: Per §11.3/Journey C: elite, honest craft — specific company/role hook in paragraph 1, JD-requirement-matched evidence in paragraph 2, elegant and specific CTA in paragraph 3, non-boastful tone throughout, clean business-letter structure.
gaps.root_cause: The cover-letter generation prompt in cover_letter_agent.py produces generic content rather than pulling a specific company/role hook and JD-requirement mapping from the sourced job + career-evidence corpus, and lacks an explicit CTA-quality directive.
gaps.fix_spec: Enhance the cover-letter generation prompt/pipeline in cover_letter_agent.py to require: (1) a specific company/role hook derived from the sourced job's JD and company context, (2) paragraph 2 evidence explicitly matched to stated JD requirements, (3) a specific, elegant CTA in the closing paragraph, all while maintaining non-boastful tone per GT-5. Follow the §11.3 required content structure (header/date/recipient/salutation/3-paragraph arc/sign-off).
tests_required: pytest: business-letter structure validator (header/date/salutation/3-paragraph arc/sign-off present)
pytest: unsupported-claim rejection still enforced
pytest: role/company hook presence check (non-generic, references actual job/company fields)
pytest: CTA presence check
pytest: PDF rendering test
writer-audit: re-score output and confirm score exceeds pre-fix 60/100
pytest: approval item creation and accept/reject flow unaffected
verification_recipe: 1. Deploy fix.
2. Generate a cover letter for a real sourced job via POST /agents/cover-letter/run.
3. Capture the draft text.
4. Run writer-audit; confirm score exceeds prior 60/100.
5. Approve the item through the approval workflow.
6. Export PDF and confirm typographically clean output.
post_evidence: Live: cover craft 82/100 (beats 60); specific role/company/program hook, JD-matched grounded evidence, specific CTA, first-person throughout (voice fix), grammatical opener, honest non-boastful tone; every quantifier verbatim-grounded.

already_satisfied.expected: 0 dead primary controls on target routes (§8 threshold); §12 Gate 3.
already_satisfied.root_cause: N/A — no defect found against fresh evidence.
already_satisfied.fix_spec: N/A — no fix required; prior remediation run's wiring fixes hold under fresh verification.

2. Click every visible primary/secondary control; confirm real network action + state transition.
3. Capture console logs and network traces per route.
4. Confirm 0 dead controls, 0 console errors, 0 5xx.

already_satisfied.root_cause: N/A — no defect found against fresh evidence.

2. GET /cover-letters/{id}/pdf; confirm %PDF header and non-trivial size.

already_satisfied.title: Fake/stale/fabricated job data — none found (diversity tracked separately)
already_satisfied.observed: All 5 sampled job sourceUrls resolved to real postings (remoteok 302 redirect; 3x seek 403 bot-block, expected/consistent with Seek's scraper defenses; all confirmed as legitimate, non-fabricated listings). NOTE: single-source concentration (96% Seek) is a DIVERSITY/ceiling concern, tracked under GAP-SRC-001 — it is not evidence of fake data.
already_satisfied.expected: 0 fake/stale/fabricated job rows presented as current (§8 threshold); §12 Gate 7.
already_satisfied.root_cause: N/A — no authenticity defect found against fresh evidence; diversity ceiling is GAP-SRC-001.
already_satisfied.fix_spec: N/A — no fix required for authenticity; see GAP-SRC-001 for diversity/volume remediation.
verification_recipe: 1. Sample jobs from GET /jobs.
2. Resolve each sourceUrl against the live source.
3. Confirm each resolves to a real, matching posting (redirect or bot-block acceptable per §10.4).

already_satisfied.title: Wireframe fidelity gaps — none found
already_satisfied.observed: All 17 wireframes in design/screens/ are implemented and mapped to their corresponding production routes/components; fidelity audit found no unmapped or missing wireframe elements.
already_satisfied.expected: Wireframe fidelity matrix completed for all 17 screens (§12 Gate 4).
already_satisfied.root_cause: N/A — no defect found against fresh evidence.
already_satisfied.fix_spec: N/A — no fix required; prior remediation run's wireframe implementation holds under fresh verification.

2. Map each to its production route/component.
3. Confirm each wireframe element has a matching implemented control.

already_satisfied.title: Invalid or non-editable agent configuration — none found
already_satisfied.observed: Agent configuration is editable and persisted via GET/PUT /agents/config and /agents/providers (confirmed live and via prior run's GAP-D1 VERIFIED-CLOSED closure). All 8 agent run endpoints (/agents/{scout,fit-scorer,tailor,cover-letter,story-extractor,email,pipeline,test-run}/run) are wired and functional.
already_satisfied.expected: Agent config editable/persisted per user (§12 Gate 2/3 scope).
already_satisfied.root_cause: N/A — no defect found against fresh evidence; carried forward from prior run's closed GAP-D1.
already_satisfied.fix_spec: N/A — no fix required. NOTE: the Anthropic subscription-OAuth path within this same surface is separately flagged as non-compliant — see GAP-AUTH-001; that finding does not invalidate this configuration-editability verdict.
verification_recipe: 1. GET /agents/config; confirm current configuration returned.
2. PUT a config change; confirm persistence on subsequent GET.
3. Confirm /agents/providers lists available providers correctly.
